D5 Design L4 Analyze Reference

Simultaneous Contrast

one grey, pasted onto four different fields — and four different colours seem to look back at you

Chevreul named the law; Itten made it a design principle. A colour casts its complement onto whatever sits within it, so an identical centre takes on a different tint over each surround. The field, not the pigment, is doing the work.

Raise the surround saturation and the four identical grey centres drift apart, each toward its field's complement. Hit reveal to drop all four onto one neutral ground — and watch them snap back to being one and the same grey.
